rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID FRANK PHILLIPS II
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/1/24 (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice of this hearing has been filed. (3) Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) It has already been more than the 90 days from the date of the filing of this report of status, and no petition for final distribution has been filed as alleged in the report. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/22/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Note to the prior attorney for the executor: Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed.)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JESSE BYRD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Modify Court Order
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The proof of service filed on 12/29/23 was incomplete at item 4a (date of mailing). Instead of filing a new proof of service, the attorney attached the same proof of service to his First Supplement filed on 3/22/24 with the date of mailing written in and without a new, current verification. 2) The current instructions for how to appear remotely should have been attached to the Notice of Hearing. On 3/29/23 [sic], the attorney reportedly mailed the instructions "Re: Byrd 3100 Petition" (not this petition to modify an order) to most of the interested parties. a) If the updated instructions were mailed on 3/29/24, that was not at least 15 days before this hearing. b) Jesse Byrd was not on the list of recipients. Nor was his guardian ad litem. c) Several of the addresses are not those shown on page 5 of the petition. d) The remote appearance instructions were apparently sent to several people together at one address, not separately to each. [CRC 7.51(a)(3).] 3) The proposed order appears to be the same proposed order that was submitted for the initial petition, not this petition to modify the order dated 7/20/23. 4) Neither this petition nor the supplement to the petition is signed or verified by the petitioner. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHEPPARD M. LEVINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The attorney's declaration on file authenticating the original UBS account statements did not include the retention language required by LRSF 14.59(A) ("original document(s) will be available for inspection in the same way as documents are maintained under CRC 2.257"). 2) The estate paid $97.08 to the attorney for reimbursement of Westlaw research costs. See LRSF 14.57(B)(legal research costs are absorbed as part of statutory fees). 3) No inventory and appraisal has been filed for 12 treasury securities the decedent held in his TreasuryDirect account at the time of his death in July 2022. The administrator's final account reported those treasury securities as additional property received ($184,730.27) in March 2023 without the probate referee's appraisal. 4) The administrator paid $10,032.50 out of the estate to a third party to prepare her final account. See LRSF 14.51 ("The Court may deduct from the personal representative's statutory commission any sums paid from estate funds for performance of the representative's ordinary duties such as ordinary accounting and bookkeeping services, including the preparation of schedules for court accounts"). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GEORGE DAVID LIVING TRUST, DATED 7/7/22
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/15/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The ONLY correct form of notice for a petition - or an amended petition or an amendment to a petition - is the mandatory Judicial Council form, on the face of which the title of the pleading to be heard must be accurately set forth and on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to it. (2) A Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Notice is defective if mailed before the pleading requiring notice is e-filed. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The amended petition was e-filed (available for viewing) as of 2/23/24. (4) A beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who is NOT a trust beneficiary and who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, must be personally served notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] (5) Why is Robert Beles missing from the list of beneficiaries? Did he predecease the grantor? If so, when, and was he survived by issue ("descendants")? He, or they if he is deceased, must receive notice. (6) How are the cousins the closest generation of heirs? Who were their common ancestors with the grantor and when did they die? (7) The prayer includes 491 shares of Kyndryl stock, not 4,091 shares. If the greater amount is correct, an amendment to the amended petition must be filed and notice of the amendment must be served after the amendment is e-filed. Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/6/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. After procedural issues are addressed, an appearance might still be required because of Probate Code Sections 21362 and 21380. ("Beneficiary" is not a relationship. One is either kin or a stranger.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CARTE REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Redress Of Breach Of Trust, Removal Of Trustee, For Recovery Of Damages, For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/14/2024. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) At least 30 days before the time set for hearing on the petition, the petitioner shall cause notice of the hearing to be delivered pursuant to Section 1215 to all trustees and all beneficiaries (Probate Code Section 17203). Form DE-120 is the mandatory form required for this proceeding. 2) All notices must include language about remote appearances (LRSF 14.1.C.6). 3) A petition involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries (CRC 7.902). 4) No proposed order has been received (LRSF 14.7.A). Please provide a proposed order, courtesy copies of the petition and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner no later than April 30, 2024.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/7/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of publication has been filed. (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (4) All persons mentioned in the will must be listed in item 8 of the petition and must receive notice, including any contingent beneficiary or alternate executor. (5) The petition caption does not disclose that IAEA authority is being requested. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies to the assigned examiner by 4/26/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MANGELS STEFFENS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing on close of estate will be set for 05/06/2025.
